generatedSentence: The Rocketeer, a peculiar mariner, enjoyed a dreamlike upswing in his windowed, elevated watchtower, observing nature's colors. His workbox held stickers and old nickels, while a canary, his handler, would apprise him of the faintest anomalies on notepads, seeking particular knowledge, relatively complete, though everywhere, a single branch reached vertically past his pleasant view. Suddenly, a dreadful, impetuous darkness descended, an octopus-like shadow that overtly hijacked the sky, creating absolute mayhem. Its wavery, fragile limbs, energized by a soviet dynamo, targeted the watchtower, instancing an abject fear in the heart of the watchman, who quickly grabbed his inhaler, observing the infantry riders below, completely unchained. The nobleman, a veteran, found himself under attack, his invincible spirit irritated as the octopus mocked him, its trick creating an appalling, nonlinear reality. He knew this was destined for epic trials; with binoculars, he saw the colorful, rambunctious pigs and turkeys, their handler a sanctimonious lawyer who seemed to facilitate this widespread deterioration, making the veteran unhappy. With a resolute click, the veteran, a dynamo himself, deployed his colorful fireworks, bathing the scene in vibrant hues, curing the widespread dread and neutralizing the threat. The few survivors, invigorated by vitamins and juice, found fellowship in the aftermath, leaving the ickiest memories behind, ready for a new reception, a truly energizing resolution.
panelSentences: The Rocketeer, a peculiar mariner, enjoyed a dreamlike upswing in his windowed, elevated watchtower, observing nature's colors. His workbox held stickers and old nickels, while a canary, his handler, would apprise him of the faintest anomalies on notepads, seeking particular knowledge, relatively complete, though everywhere, a single branch reached vertically past his pleasant view.

Suddenly, a dreadful, impetuous darkness descended, an octopus-like shadow that overtly hijacked the sky, creating absolute mayhem. Its wavery, fragile limbs, energized by a soviet dynamo, targeted the watchtower, instancing an abject fear in the heart of the watchman, who quickly grabbed his inhaler, observing the infantry riders below, completely unchained.

The nobleman, a veteran, found himself under attack, his invincible spirit irritated as the octopus mocked him, its trick creating an appalling, nonlinear reality. He knew this was destined for epic trials; with binoculars, he saw the colorful, rambunctious pigs and turkeys, their handler a sanctimonious lawyer who seemed to facilitate this widespread deterioration, making the veteran unhappy.

With a resolute click, the veteran, a dynamo himself, deployed his colorful fireworks, bathing the scene in vibrant hues, curing the widespread dread and neutralizing the threat. The few survivors, invigorated by vitamins and juice, found fellowship in the aftermath, leaving the ickiest memories behind, ready for a new reception, a truly energizing resolution.
rawPanelSentences: The Rocketeer, a peculiar mariner, enjoyed a dreamlike upswing in his windowed, elevated watchtower, observing nature's colors. His workbox held stickers and old nickels, while a canary, his handler, would apprise him of the faintest anomalies on notepads, seeking particular knowledge, relatively complete, though everywhere, a single branch reached vertically past his pleasant view.

Suddenly, a dreadful, impetuous darkness descended, an octopus-like shadow that overtly hijacked the sky, creating absolute mayhem. Its wavery, fragile limbs, energized by a soviet dynamo, targeted the watchtower, instancing an abject fear in the heart of the watchman, who quickly grabbed his inhaler, observing the infantry riders below, completely unchained.

The nobleman, a veteran, found himself under attack, his invincible spirit irritated as the octopus mocked him, its trick creating an appalling, nonlinear reality. He knew this was destined for epic trials; with binoculars, he saw the colorful, rambunctious pigs and turkeys, their handler a sanctimonious lawyer who seemed to facilitate this widespread deterioration, making the veteran unhappy.

With a resolute click, the veteran, a dynamo himself, deployed his colorful fireworks, bathing the scene in vibrant hues, curing the widespread dread and neutralizing the threat. The few survivors, invigorated by vitamins and juice, found fellowship in the aftermath, leaving the ickiest memories behind, ready for a new reception, a truly energizing resolution.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A grizzled veteran in a sleek, silver-chrome space suit, with a distinctive gold star emblem on his chest. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The veteran Rocketeer sits at a high-tech console, looking out a panoramic window at a peaceful, alien landscape, a canary perched on his shoulder, warm sunlight streaming in. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A colossal, shadowy octopus-like entity with glowing red eyes descends from a storm-filled sky, its tentacles reaching menacingly towards the watchtower, distant silhouettes of armored riders on the ground. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The veteran, face grim and determined, uses high-tech binoculars to observe a chaotic scene below: grotesque, rambunctious alien pigs and turkeys running amok, overseen by a smirking, cloaked figure. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The veteran stands triumphant amidst a burst of colorful, futuristic fireworks illuminating the night sky, the defeated octopus-entity shrinking in the distance, a small group of figures below cheering, bathed in neon glow.

definitions.definition: A person in the lowest and most despicable condition; a castaway; outcast.
Sunk to or existing in a low condition, state, or position.
Cast down in spirit or hope; degraded; servile; grovelling; despicable; lacking courage; offered in a humble and often ingratiating spirit.
Showing utter hopelessness, helplessness; showing resignation; wretched.
Rejected; cast aside.
A small individual part of something larger; a detail, a point.
A person's own individual case.
(chiefly in plural) A particular case; an individual thing as opposed to a whole class. (Opposed to generals, universals.)
Pertaining only to a part of something; partial.
Specific; discrete; concrete.
definitions.example: I couldn't find the particular model you asked for, but I hope this one will do.

definitions.definition: Specialised; characteristic of a specific person or thing.
definitions.example: I don't appreciate your particular brand of cynicism.

definitions.definition: Known only to an individual person or group; confidential.
Distinguished in some way; special (often in negative constructions).
definitions.example: He brought no particular news.

definitions.definition: Of a person, concerned with, or attentive to, details; minute; precise; fastidious.
definitions.example: He is very particular about his food and if it isn't cooked to perfection he will send it back.

definitions.definition: Concerned with, or attentive to, details; minute; circumstantial; precise.
definitions.example: a full and particular account of an accident

definitions.definition: Containing a part only; limited.
definitions.example: a particular estate, or one precedent to an estate in remainder

definitions.definition: Holding a particular estate.
definitions.example: a particular tenant

definitions.definition: Forming a part of a genus; relatively limited in extension; affirmed or denied of a part of a subject.
definitions.example: a particular proposition, opposed to "universal", e.g. (particular affirmative) "Some men are wise"; (particular negative) "Some men are not wise".

definitions.definition: To invigorate; to make energetic.
To supply with energy, especially electricity; to turn on power to (something).
definitions.example: Whenever we energize that circuit we blow a fuse.

definitions.definition: To use strength in action; to act or operate with force or vigor; to act in producing an effect.
In or to all locations under discussion.
definitions.example: He delivers the mail everywhere on this street.

definitions.definition: In or to a few or more locations.
definitions.example: I've looked everywhere in the house and still can't find my glasses.

definitions.definition: A person without religious affiliation.
To no extent, in no way.
definitions.example: I felt none the worse for my recent illness.

definitions.definition: Not at all, not very.
definitions.example: He was none too pleased with the delays in the program that was supposed to be his legacy.

definitions.definition: No, not.
Not any of a given number or group.
definitions.example: None of this meat tastes right.

definitions.definition: A silvery elemental metal with an atomic number of 28 and symbol Ni.
A coin worth 5 cents.
(by extension) Five dollars.
(by extension) Five hundred dollars.
(sometimes the nickel or the hot nickel) Interstate 5, a highway that runs along the west coast of the United States.
A playing card with the rank of five
A five-year prison sentence.
A defensive formation with five defensive backs, one of whom is a nickelback, instead of the more common four.
To plate with nickel.
The spectral composition of visible light
definitions.example: Humans and birds can perceive color.

definitions.definition: A subset thereof:
A paint.
definitions.example: The artist took out her colors and began work on a landscape.

definitions.definition: Human skin tone, especially as an indicator of race or ethnicity.
definitions.example: Color has been a sensitive issue in many societies.

definitions.definition: Skin color, noted as normal, jaundiced, cyanotic, flush, mottled, pale, or ashen as part of the skin signs assessment.
A flushed appearance of blood in the face; redness of complexion.
Richness of expression; detail or flavour that is likely to generate interest or enjoyment.
definitions.example: Could you give me some color with regards to which products made up the mix of revenue for this quarter?

definitions.definition: A standard, flag, or insignia:
(in the plural) An award for sporting achievement, particularly within a school or university.
definitions.example: He was awarded colors for his football.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) The morning ceremony of raising the flag.
A property of quarks, with three values called red, green, and blue, which they can exchange by passing gluons.

A third-order measure of derivative price sensitivity, expressed as the rate of change of gamma with respect to time, or equivalently the rate of change of charm with respect to changes in the underlying asset price.
The relative lightness or darkness of a mass of written or printed text on a page. (See type color.)
Any of the colored balls excluding the reds.
A front or facade; an ostensible truth actually false; pretext.
An appearance of right or authority; color of law.
definitions.example: Under color of law, he managed to bilk taxpayers of millions of dollars.

definitions.definition: To give something color.
definitions.example: We could color the walls red.

definitions.definition: To apply colors to the areas within the boundaries of a line drawing using colored markers or crayons.
definitions.example: My kindergartener loves to color.

definitions.definition: (of a person or their face) To become red through increased blood flow.
definitions.example: Her face colored as she realized her mistake.

definitions.definition: To affect without completely changing.
definitions.example: That interpretation certainly colors my perception of the book.

definitions.definition: To attribute a quality to; to portray (as).
definitions.example: Color me confused.

definitions.definition: To assign colors to the vertices of a graph (or the regions of a map) so that no two vertices connected by an edge (regions sharing a border) have the same color.
definitions.example: Can this graph be 2-colored?

definitions.definition: A sailor.
Like something from a dream; having a sense of vagueness, insubstantiality, or incongruousness.
definitions.example: Her kiss sent me into a dreamlike state of bliss, and while we must have spent the next several hours wandering the fairground together, I can hardly remember any of it.

definitions.definition: An electricity generator, a dynamo-electric machine.
An energetic person.
definitions.example: That new Vice President of Marketing is a real dynamo; sales have already gone up this quarter.

definitions.definition: In an overt manner; publicly; openly.
Any of several marine molluscs of the family Octopodidae, having no internal or external protective shell or bone (unlike the nautilus, squid and cuttlefish) and eight arms each covered with suckers.
The flesh of these marine molluscs eaten as food.
An organization that has many powerful branches controlled from the centre.
To put (or attempt to put) one's fingers, hands or arms in many things or places at roughly the same time.
To spread out in long arms or legs in many directions.
To plug a large number of devices into a single electric outlet.
(by extension) To grow in use vastly beyond what was originally intended.
To hunt and catch octopuses.
To behave like an octopus.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
Of a vehicle, aircraft, vessel, computer, etc.: whose control has been seized by force
An extended narrative poem in elevated or dignified language, celebrating the feats of a deity, demigod (heroic epic), other legend or traditional hero.
definitions.example: The Icelandic epic took all night to recite.

definitions.definition: A series of events considered appropriate to an epic.
definitions.example: The book was an epic in four volumes.

definitions.definition: In software development, a large or extended user story.
Of, or relating to, an epic.
definitions.example: Beowulf is an epic poem.

definitions.definition: Momentously heroic; grand in scale or character
definitions.example: The epic defense was rewarded with the highest military decorations

definitions.definition: Extending beyond the usual or ordinary.
definitions.example: The after-prom party was truly epic.

definitions.definition: To raise (something) to a higher position.

definitions.definition: To promote (someone) to a higher rank.
To confer honor or nobility on (someone).

definitions.definition: To make (something or someone) more worthy or of greater value.

To increase the intensity or degree of (something).

definitions.definition: To lift the spirits of (someone)
To intoxicate in a slight degree; to make (someone) tipsy.
(Latinism) To attempt to make (something) seem less important, remarkable, etc.
An elevated railway.
Raised, particularly above ground level.
Increased, particularly above a normal level.
definitions.example: The patient presented with elevated blood pressure.

definitions.definition: Of a higher rank or status.
Running with administration rights granted
definitions.example: Install all the required tools from an elevated console.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated; drunk
An upright standing or hanging metal bowl used for holding burning coal for a source of light or heat.
A worker in brass.
Instantly appealing and memorable (of a tune or phrase).
Tending to catch or ensnare; entangling.

definitions.definition: Consisting of, or occurring in, disconnected parts or snatches; changeable.
definitions.example: a catchy wind

definitions.definition: The action of mocking; ridicule, derision.
Something so lacking in necessary qualities as to inspire ridicule; a laughing-stock.
Something insultingly imitative; an offensively futile action, gesture etc.
Mimicry, imitation, now usually in a derogatory sense; a travesty, a ridiculous simulacrum.

definitions.definition: To preordain
To assign something (especially finance) for a particular use
To have a particular destination
Confined to a predetermined fate or destiny; certain.

definitions.definition: The state of being dark; lack of light.

The product of being dark.
The state or quality of reflecting little light, of tending to a blackish or brownish color.

definitions.definition: Evilness, lack of understanding or compassion, reference to death or suffering.
Somebody who designs, launches, operates, or travels in a rocket.
One who inhales.
A device with a canister holding medicine (either in powder or gas form) which is sprayed and inhaled by the patient, often for treating asthma and other respiratory diseases.
Something or someone that sticks.

An adhesive label or decal.
A price tag.

definitions.definition: A cartoonish illustration of a character that represents an emotion or action, often accompanied by text, that may be superimposed on a digital image.
A burr or seed pod that catches in fur or clothing.
That which causes one to stick; that which puzzles or poses.
A wooden strip placed between courses of lumber to allow air circulation (also kiln sticker).
A small wooden rod in an organ which connects (in part) a key and a pallet, so as to communicate motion by pushing.
A brand, label, or company, especially one making and distributing records.
A paster.
In a vertical direction or position.
A wit; a humorist; a buffoon.
Giving pleasure; pleasing in manner.

definitions.definition: Facetious, joking.
The fact of knowing about something; general understanding or familiarity with a subject, place, situation etc.

Intellectual understanding; the state of appreciating truth or information.

Sexual intimacy or intercourse (now usually in phrase carnal knowledge).
Information or intelligence about something; notice.
The total of what is known; all information and products of learning.

Acknowledgement.
Notice, awareness.
The deep familiarity with certain routes and places of interest required by taxicab drivers working in London, England.
To confess as true; to acknowledge.

Blood relationship.
Affinity, likeness.
A household or group following the modern pagan faith of Heathenry or √Åsatr√∫.
Of the same nature, or of similar character.
Connected, related, cognate, akin.

definitions.definition: To furnish with windows.
To place at or in a window.
Fitted with windows (often of a particular kind).

definitions.definition: Being lower; being beneath something.

definitions.definition: In a way lower or less than.
In a way inferior to.
In an unconscious state.
definitions.example: It took the hypnotist several minutes to make his subject go under.

definitions.definition: In or at a lower level than.
As a subject of; subordinate to.
definitions.example: He served in World War II under General Omar Bradley.

definitions.definition: Less than.
Below the surface of.
In the face of; in response to (some attacking force).

definitions.definition: As, in the character of.
definitions.example: he writes books under the name John Smith

definitions.definition: To cause to feel fear; to scare; to cause to feel alarm or fright.
definitions.example: Avery puts a sheet over her head, pretending to be a ghost to frighten Emily.

definitions.definition: Possessing prominent and varied colors.
Interesting, multifaceted, energetic, distinctive.
Profane, obscene, offensive (usually in the phrase colourful language).
A state or situation of great confusion, disorder, trouble or destruction; chaos.

The crime of damaging things or harming people on purpose.
To make worse; to make inferior in quality or value; to impair.

Unpleasantly sticky; yucky; disgusting.

definitions.definition: Excessively sentimental.
Unwell or upset; in a bad state of mind or health.

definitions.definition: One who survives, especially one who survives a traumatic experience.
definitions.example: From this moment on, I choose to live not as a victim, but as a survivor.

definitions.definition: A person who is able to endure hardship.
One who knew a specific decedent.
definitions.example: She was from a large family and had many friends, so the funeral was crowded with mourning survivors.

definitions.definition: Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.
To extend the index finger in the direction of something in order to show where it is or to draw attention to it.
definitions.example: It's rude to point at other people.

definitions.definition: To draw attention to something or indicate a direction.
definitions.example: The arrow of a compass points north

definitions.definition: To face in a particular direction.
(sometimes figurative) To direct toward an object; to aim.
definitions.example: to point a gun at a wolf, or a cannon at a fort

definitions.definition: To give a point to; to sharpen; to cut, forge, grind, or file to an acute end.

definitions.definition: To indicate a probability of something.
To repair mortar.
To fill up and finish the joints of (a wall), by introducing additional cement or mortar, and bringing it to a smooth surface.
(stone-cutting) To cut, as a surface, with a pointed tool.
To direct or encourage (someone) in a particular direction.

definitions.definition: To separate an integer from a decimal with a decimal point.
To mark with diacritics.
To supply with punctuation marks; to punctuate.

definitions.definition: To direct the central processing unit to seek information at a certain location in memory.
To direct requests sent to a domain name to the IP address corresponding to that domain name.
To sail close to the wind.
definitions.example: Bear off a little, we're pointing.

definitions.definition: To indicate the presence of game by a fixed and steady look, as certain hunting dogs do.
(of an abscess) To approximate to the surface; to head.
To give point to (something said or done); to give particular prominence or force to.
To appoint.
The action of the verb to point.
(usually singular or collective, sometimes proscribed) Mortar that has been placed between bricks to hold them together.
The act or art of punctuating; punctuation.
The rubbing off of the point of the wheat grain in the first process of high milling.

The act or process of measuring, at the various distances from the surface of a block of marble, the surface of a future piece of statuary; also, a process used in cutting the statue from the artist's model.
Land, specifically, an open field, on which livestock is kept for feeding.
Ground covered with grass or herbage, used or suitable for the grazing of livestock.
Food, nourishment.
To move animals into a pasture.
To graze.
To feed, especially on growing grass; to supply grass as food for.
definitions.example: The farmer pastures fifty oxen; the land will pasture forty cows.

definitions.definition: Tending to waver; uncertain or hesitant.
A company of people that share the same interest or aim.
definitions.example: The Fellowship of the Ring

definitions.definition: Company, companions; a group of people or things following another.
A feeling of friendship, relatedness or connection between people.
A merit-based scholarship.
A temporary position at an academic institution with limited teaching duties and ample time for research; this may also be called a postdoc.
A period of supervised, sub-specialty medical training in the United States and Canada that a physician may undertake after completing a specialty training program or residency.
The proportional division of profit and loss among partners.
To admit to fellowship, enter into fellowship with; to make feel welcome by showing friendship or building a cordial relationship. Now only in religious use.
definitions.example: The Bishop's family fellowshipped the new converts.

definitions.definition: To join in fellowship; to associate with.
definitions.example: After she got married, she stopped fellowshipping with the singles in our church.

definitions.definition: To express sadness or sorrow for; to grieve over (especially a death).
To utter in a sorrowful manner.
To wear mourning.
The act of expressing or feeling sorrow or regret; lamentation.
Feeling or expressing sorrow over someone's death.
The traditional clothes worn by those who mourn (in Western societies, typically coloured black).
Drapes or coverings associated with mourning.
The guinea fowl (family Numididae).
A bird in the genus Meleagris with a fan-shaped tail and wattled neck, especially the wild turkey (Meleagris gallopavo, now domesticated).
The flesh or meat of this bird eaten as food.
definitions.example: All week after Thanksgiving, I had turkey sandwiches for lunch.

definitions.definition: With a distinguishing word: a bird resembling the Meleagris gallopavo (for example, the bush turkey or brush turkey (Alectura lathami), and the water turkey (Anhinga anhinga)).
An act of throwing three strikes in a row.
A patient feigning symptoms; a person faking illness or injury; a malingerer.
A pack carried by a lumberman; a bindle; also, a large travel bag, a suitcase.
A failure.
definitions.example: That film was a turkey.

definitions.definition: (usually mildly) A foolish or inept person.
definitions.example: The turkey cut in front of me and then berated me for running into him.

definitions.definition: To mention as a case or example; to refer to; to cite
To cite an example as proof; to exemplify.
A toolbox; a container for the items needed to carry out a task.
A set of skills and approaches.

A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and sometimes made to rocket high into the sky before exploding, used for entertainment or celebration.
definitions.example: Commercial firework displays are commonly launched from boats or other vessels

definitions.definition: An event or a display where fireworks are set off.
A boisterous or violent event or situation.
definitions.example: I left the room after John came home drunk but before the fireworks went off.

definitions.definition: A strip of cloth worn around the neck and tied in the front. See also bowtie.
A small, usually yellow, finch (genus Serinus), a songbird native to the Canary Islands.
Any of various small birds of different countries, most of which are largely yellow in colour.
A light, slightly greenish, yellow colour.
A light, sweet, white wine from the Canary Islands.
A lively dance, possibly of Spanish origin (also called canaries).
Any test subject, especially an inadvertent or unwilling one. (From the mining practice of using canaries to detect dangerous gases.)
A value placed in memory such that it will be the first data corrupted by a buffer overflow, allowing the program to identify and recover from it.
A female singer, soprano, a coloratura singer.
An informer or snitch; a squealer.
A (usually yellow) capsule of the short-acting barbiturate pentobarbital/pentobarbitone (Nembutal).
A yellow sticker of unroadworthiness.
To dance nimbly (as in the canary dance)
To inform or snitch, to betray secrets, especially about illegal activities.
Of a light yellow colour.
A group of twelve items.
definitions.example: Fractions would be a little easier if we counted by twelves.

definitions.definition: A twelve-bore gun.
A jury (normally composed of twelve persons).
Police; law enforcement, especially a narcotics officer
A substance that does not transmit heat (thermal insulator), sound (acoustic insulator) or electricity (electrical insulator).
A non-conductive structure, coating or device that does not transmit sound, heat or electricity (see image)
definitions.example: To isolate electrical wires from the pylons supporting them, one often uses glass insulators.

definitions.definition: A person who installs insulation.
Making a show of being morally better than others, especially hypocritically pious.
Holy, devout.
An observation tower in which a lookout keeps watch over prisoners, or looks out for fires, etc.
An undergarment worn beneath a shirt, often collarless and sleeveless.
Treating serious issues with (often deliberately) inappropriate humour; flippant.
definitions.example: Robbie's joke about Heather's appearance was just him being facetious.

definitions.definition: Pleasantly humorous; jocular.
A peer; an aristocrat; ranks range from baron to king to emperor.
A nocturnal omnivore native to North America, typically with a mixture of gray, brown, and black fur, a mask-like marking around the eyes and a striped tail; Procyon lotor.
Any mammal of the genus Procyon.
Any mammal of the subfamily Procyoninae, a procyonine.
Any mammal of the family Procyonidae, a procyonid.
A person or business that sells or leases out real estate, acting as an agent for the property owner.
Soldiers who fight on foot (on land), as opposed to cavalry and other mounted units, regardless of external transport (e.g. airborne).
The part of an army consisting of infantry soldiers, especially opposed to mounted and technical troops
A regiment of infantry
A cue sport, popular in the UK and other Commonwealth of Nations countries.
The situation where the cue ball is in such a position that the opponent cannot directly hit the required ball with it.
To play the game of snooker.
To fool or bamboozle.
